concerns.text: Task T048 mandates implementing a 'Physics-Informed Regularization' term in the MLP. This alters the model architecture defined in FR-005 ('2-layer MLP regression model') and Constitution Principle VII ('downstream regression model must be a 2-layer MLP'). The spec does not authorize custom loss functions or physics-informed constraints; this is a silent relaxation of the architectural constraint and a scope creep that violates the defined model scope.

Task T053 requires implementing a 'cellular automaton or rewriting system to generate synthetic packing efficiencies'. This violates Constitution Principle VI (Open Crystallographic Data Integrity) which mandates all records be obtained from COD, and Principle IV (Single Source of Truth). The spec (FR-001 to FR-019) does not provide a mechanism for generating synthetic data; this task introduces an external data source not sanctioned by the spec or plan.

Tasks T049-T052 mandate extracting and using 'measured diffraction parameters' (unit-cell dimensions, angles, intensity profiles) and 'X-ray powder diffraction measurements'. The spec (FR-002, FR-011) explicitly limits the target to CAPE derived from unit-cell volume and van der Waals sums. The plan and spec do not define a mechanism to handle 'modest set of X-ray powder diffraction measurements' which are not guaranteed in COD metadata. This introduces a data availability gap and potential violation of the 'Single Source of Truth' if synthetic or external data is used to fill the gap.

Task T027 includes a fallback to '[deferred]' if the 10,000-shuffle permutation test runtime exceeds 4h. FR-006 and FR-016 mandate 10,000 shuffles. SC-002 requires the Bonferroni-corrected p-value ≤ 0.05 based on this test. A fallback to a lower number of shuffles (implied by '[deferred]' or runtime reduction) violates the mandatory success criteria and the specific requirement for 10,000 shuffles, weakening the statistical validation constraint.

Task T028 specifies VIF diagnostics on 'PCA-reduced fingerprint + low-dim descriptors + confounders'. FR-009 explicitly requires VIF diagnostics on 'all predictor variables (fingerprint dimensions, 3-D descriptors, and recorded confounders)'. The task silently narrows the scope to PCA-reduced components, violating the requirement to check multicollinearity across the full feature set as defined in the spec.
